title: Area code 262

infobox.value: is a telephone [area code](/wiki/Area_code "Area code") in the [North American Numbering Plan](/wiki/North_American_Numbering_Plan "North American Numbering Plan") for the southeastern part of the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin"). The numbering plan area comprises [suburbs](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") that are a part of the [Milwaukee](/wiki/Milwaukee_metropolitan_area "Milwaukee metropolitan area") and [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago_metropolitan_area "Chicago metropolitan area") metropolitan areas. The area code was created on September 25, 1999, in an [area code split](/wiki/Area_code_split "Area code split") of [area code 414](/wiki/Area_code_414 "Area code 414").

["2018-2 NRUF and NPA Exhaust Analysis"](https://web.archive.org/web/20200221214515/https://nationalnanpa.com/reports/2019-2_NPA_Exhaust_Projections_Final.pdf) (PDF). [North American Numbering Plan](/wiki/North_American_Numbering_Plan "North American Numbering Plan") Administration. Archived from [the original](https://nationalnanpa.com/reports/2019-2_NPA_Exhaust_Projections_Final.pdf) (PDF) on February 21, 2020. Retrieved February 2, 2020.

["Transition to 10-digit dialing (for 988 as 3-digit access to National Suicide Prevention Hotline)"](https://nationalnanpa.com/pdf/PL_556.pdf) (PDF). North American Numbering Plan Administrator. January 8, 2021. Retrieved March 9, 2021.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [43°00′N 88°15′W﻿ / ﻿43.000°N 88.250°W﻿ / 43.000; -88.250](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Area_code_262&params=43_00_N_88_15_W_region:US-WI_scale:1000000)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Area code in southeastern Wisconsin

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/69/WI_Area_Codes.svg/250px-WI_Area_Codes.svg.png)](/wiki/File:WI_Area_Codes.svg)

Map of Wisconsin's numbering plan areas and area codes

**Area code 262** is a telephone [area code](/wiki/Area_code "Area code") in the [North American Numbering Plan](/wiki/North_American_Numbering_Plan "North American Numbering Plan") for the southeastern part of the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin"). The numbering plan area comprises [suburbs](/wiki/Suburb "Suburb") that are a part of the [Milwaukee](/wiki/Milwaukee_metropolitan_area "Milwaukee metropolitan area") and [Chicago](/wiki/Chicago_metropolitan_area "Chicago metropolitan area") metropolitan areas. The area code was created on September 25, 1999, in an [area code split](/wiki/Area_code_split "Area code split") of [area code 414](/wiki/Area_code_414 "Area code 414").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Area_code_262&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The eastern portion of Wisconsin had been served by area code 414 for half a century until the [Green Bay](/wiki/Green_Bay,_Wisconsin "Green Bay, Wisconsin") and the [Fox River Valley](/wiki/Fox_Cities "Fox Cities") were designated a new numbering plan area (NPA) with [area code 920](/wiki/Area_code_920 "Area code 920") in 1997, to provide more telephone numbers in the densest populated area of Milwaukee County. Although this was intended as a long-term solution, within a year 414 was again approaching exhaustion due to the continued proliferation of cell phones and pagers. The original mitigation plans called for 262 to be added as an [overlay code](/wiki/Overlay_complex "Overlay complex") for all of southeastern Wisconsin. However, overlays were a new concept at the time, and met with some resistance to the mixing of area codes in the same area and the ensuing requirement for [ten-digit dialing](/wiki/Ten-digit_dialing "Ten-digit dialing"). As a result, 262 was designated for a separate numbering plan area with nearly all of the territory outside Milwaukee County. A few slivers of [Waukesha](/wiki/Waukesha_County,_Wisconsin "Waukesha County, Wisconsin") and [Washington](/wiki/Washington_County,_Wisconsin "Washington County, Wisconsin") counties stayed in 414.

Projections in early 2019 suggested the Milwaukee suburbs would not need another area code until 2044 at the earliest, but later in 2019 no exhaust date was listed, meaning the date is 30 years or more in the future.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Prior to October 2021, area code 262 had telephone numbers assigned for the [central office code](/wiki/Central_office_code "Central office code") 988. In 2020, *988* was designated nationwide as a dialing code for the [National Suicide Prevention Lifeline](/wiki/National_Suicide_Prevention_Lifeline "National Suicide Prevention Lifeline"), which created a conflict for exchanges that permit [seven-digit dialing](/wiki/Seven-digit_dialing "Seven-digit dialing"). This area code was therefore scheduled to transition to [ten-digit dialing](/wiki/Ten-digit_dialing "Ten-digit dialing") by October 24, 2021.[[2]](#cite_note-pl556-2)

## Service area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Area_code_262&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Service area")]

The area code serves the following six counties:

:   [Kenosha](/wiki/Kenosha_County,_Wisconsin "Kenosha County, Wisconsin"), [Ozaukee](/wiki/Ozaukee_County,_Wisconsin "Ozaukee County, Wisconsin"), [Racine](/wiki/Racine_County,_Wisconsin "Racine County, Wisconsin"), [Walworth](/wiki/Walworth_County,_Wisconsin "Walworth County, Wisconsin"), [Washington](/wiki/Washington_County,_Wisconsin "Washington County, Wisconsin") (shared with 414), and [Waukesha](/wiki/Waukesha_County,_Wisconsin "Waukesha County, Wisconsin") (shared with 414). Kenosha County and all its cities are part of the [Chicago metropolitan area](/wiki/Chicago_metropolitan_area "Chicago metropolitan area") while the cities in the other counties are part of the [Milwaukee metropolitan area](/wiki/Milwaukee_metropolitan_area "Milwaukee metropolitan area").

These counties include the municipalities of
